questions.statement:
<p>Choose the ANTONYM of the given word.</p>

<p>MALAISE</p>

<p>In the following question, out of the given alternatives, choose the one which is most nearly the same in meaning to the underlined word in the sentence:</p>

<p><u>Sporadic</u> rise in his body temperature has&nbsp;caused us much worry.</p>

<p><strong>Direction:</strong> <strong>Each expression is followed by four words. Select the word you think best conveys the idea.</strong></p>

<p>A schoolboy who cuts classes frequently is a:-</p>

<p style=""><span style="font-weight: 700;">Direction: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.</span></p>

<p style="">Against The Clock</p>
options: Forced to hurry to meet a deadline /
On or to the other side of the Atlantic Ocean. /

questions.statement:
<p><strong><span style=" line-height: 107%;  background-image: initial; background-position: initial; background-size: initial; background-repeat: initial; background-attachment: initial; background-origin: initial; background-clip: initial;">Direction:&nbsp;In the questions given below out of four alternatives, choose the one which can be substituted for the given word/sentence.</span></strong></p>

<p>One who is unable to read or write</p>

<p><strong>Direction: Select the most appropriate synonym for the underlined word in the given sentence.</strong></p>

<div>When the owner stopped paying the workers, a lot of them went <strong><u>rogue</u></strong>.</div>

<p>Select the most appropriate word for the given group of words.</p>

<p>License granted to an individual or group to market a company&#39;s goods or services in a particular territory</p>

<p>Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word.</p>

<div>Grandeur</div>

<p><strong>Choose the word which is nearly opposite in meaning to the given word</strong>:-</p>

<p>Sceptic</p>

<p><span style="font-weight: 700;">Direction: Choose the correct one word substitution from the given options :</span></p>

<p>Something kept as a reminder of an event :&nbsp;</p>

<p><strong>Directions: Choose the word or phrase which is an antonym or the most opposite in meaning to the word or phrase given below and mark the correct answer.</strong></p>

<p>Mandatory</p>

<p><strong>Give a one-word substitute for the following.</strong></p>

<p>Beyond the power or laws of nature.</p>

<p style=""><span style="font-weight: 700;">Direction: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.</span></p>

<p style="">Write off</p>
options: Accept or set aside a disagreement /
Remove from a business record, cancel a debt /
In a negotiation, an agreement in which not all details have been worked out /
Making faster progress than anticipated; ahead of schedule /

questions.statement:
<p><strong>Choose the word that is opposite in meaning to the given word.</strong></p>

<p>Bicker</p>

<p style=""><span style="font-weight: 700;">Direction:&nbsp;</span>Choose the word which is most nearly the<span style="font-weight: 700;">&nbsp;SAME&nbsp;</span>in meaning as the word printed in Bold.</p>

<p style=""><b>Impediment</b></p>

<p>Select the word which means the same as the group of words given.</p>

<p>Special words or expressions used by a professional or group that are difficult for others to understand.</p>

<p><strong>Select the antonym of the given word.</strong></p>

<p>MALEVOLENT</p>

<p><strong>Directions: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word.</strong></p>

<p>Abode</p>

<p>Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom</p>

<p>An eye wash</p>

<p><strong>Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.</strong></p>

<p>Example is better than precept</p>
options: Actions and precepts are unrelated in terms of impact. /
An example of a blockbuster movie. /
Demonstrating a plan through advising someone. /

questions.statement:
<p>Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom / phrase.</p>

<p>Kick the can down the road</p>
options: A task or job that is easy to complete&nbsp; /
It never changes back or comes back&nbsp; /
To avoid dealing with a problem&nbsp; /

questions.statement:
<p style=""><span style="font-weight: 700;">Direction:&nbsp;</span>Choose the word which is most nearly the&nbsp;<span style="font-weight: 700;">SAME&nbsp;</span>in the meaning as the word printed in&nbsp;<span style="font-weight: 700;">Bold</span>.</p>

<p style=""><span style="font-weight: 700;">Incumbent</span></p>

<p style=""><span style="font-weight: 700;">Direction: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.</span></p>

<p style="">Zero in on something&nbsp;</p>
options: To focus all your attention on a particular thing /
Forced to hurry to meet a deadline /

questions.statement: What does the word &quot;replenish&quot; mean?

<p><strong>Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom / phrase.</strong></p>

<p>To take to one&#39;s heels&nbsp;</p>
